violations.description: Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooling of potentially hazardous food was not accomplished using proper methods. -Texas sauce in loosely covered large plastic container was left out at room temperature for ~1 hour and then moved to walk in cooler to cool. *Found with internal temperature of 130 and edge temperature of 90F. *Directed to stir and ice bath product to expedite cooling. Staff moved product to freezer for faster cooling.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Seasoning/residue build up on warmer handle. *COS-cleaned immediately. -Spill/residue at bottom interior of oven.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not covered or inverted during storage. -Clean bowls stored on top of warmer were not inverted. -Clean foil pans on shelf above/near steam well were not inverted. *COS-Staff inverted dishes while on-site.

Water Supply. — --Nonpublic water supply was not tested by EPA-certified laboratory annually for nitrate analysis. -Documentation of water samples not available at time of inspection (thought to have been done ~3 months ago).--Nonpublic water supply was not tested by EPA-certified laboratory monthly for bacteriological analysis. -Documentation of water samples not available at time of inspection (thought to have been done ~3 months ago). *Staff have sample bottles available and intend on submitting samples next day, 7/8/26.
